series.title: [PATCH] ide: Fix potential assertion failure on VM stop for PIO

review.summary: The change is correct and narrowly scoped. `ide_sector_read()` can start block I/O before `ide_transfer_start()` runs, so moving `ide_set_retry()` into the read path closes the stale-retry window described in the commit message. I did not identify a behavioral regression from the added call, and the patch matches the existing write-side intent of recording retry state before issuing I/O.

stages.A: Patch intent is clear: prevent stale retry metadata when a PIO read request fails before `ide_transfer_start()` has a chance to record retry state.
stages.B: Code-path analysis supports the fix. For reads, I/O is initiated from `ide_sector_read()`, so setting retry state there is the right place to satisfy `ide_handle_rw_error()` expectations.
stages.C: The change is minimal and low risk: one added call before the existing zero-length check and before any read I/O submission. No ABI, interface, or unrelated control-flow changes are introduced.
stages.D: The patch is consistent with the commit message rationale that write requests are already covered by the retry setup in the transfer path, while reads are not.
stages.E: With checkpatch clean, git-am successful, and configure passing, this looks ready from a review perspective. Residual risk is limited to runtime coverage of the specific failing-read path, but I found no code-level issue in the patch itself.

diff:
From git@z Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
Subject: [PATCH] ide: Fix potential assertion failure on VM stop for PIO
 read error
From: Kevin Wolf <kwolf@redhat.com>
Date: Thu, 26 Mar 2026 17:51:24 +0100
Message-Id: <20260326165124.138593-1-kwolf@redhat.com>
MIME-Version: 1.0
Content-Type: text/plain; charset="utf-8"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit

ide_sector_read() as well as its callers neglect to call ide_set_retry()
before starting I/O. If the I/O fails, this means that the retry
information is stale. In particular, ide_handle_rw_error() has an
assertion that s->bus->retry_unit == s->unit, which can fail if either
there was no previous request or it came from another device on the bus.
If the assertion weren't there, a wrong request would be retried after
resuming the VM.

Fix this by adding a ide_set_retry() call to ide_sector_read().

This affects only reads because ide_transfer_start() does call
ide_set_retry(). For writes, the data transfer comes first and the I/O
is only started when the data has been read into s->io_buffer, so by
that time, ide_set_retry() has been called. For reads, however, the I/O
comes first and only then the data is transferred to the guest, so the
call in ide_transfer_start() is too late.

Buglink: https://redhat.atlassian.net/browse/RHEL-153537
Reported-by: Tingting Mao <timao@redhat.com>
Signed-off-by: Kevin Wolf <kwolf@redhat.com>
---
 hw/ide/core.c | 1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

diff --git a/hw/ide/core.c b/hw/ide/core.c
index b45abf067b2..52a991fc482 100644
--- a/hw/ide/core.c
+++ b/hw/ide/core.c
@@ -799,6 +799,7 @@ static void ide_sector_read(IDEState *s)
     s->error = 0; /* not needed by IDE spec, but needed by Windows */
     sector_num = ide_get_sector(s);
     n = s->nsector;
+    ide_set_retry(s);
 
     if (n == 0) {
         ide_transfer_stop(s);
-- 
2.53.0
disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
